Service Above Self

Welcome

Thank you for scanning my business card. I have spent my professional life as an engineer, author and business owner. Today my greatest passion is working with Rotary to build sustainable community projects and strengthen international friendships through service.

A lifetime of engineering has taught me how to build things. Rotary has taught me how to build communities.

Rotary in Action

🔔 Rotary Peace Bell – Albury Wodonga

Project Chairman · Project Manager · Designer

The Rotary Peace Bell Project was created to provide a permanent focal point where students, families and the wider community can come together to reflect on the importance of peace.

Not only peace between nations, but also peace within our homes, our schools, our workplaces and our communities.

May this Peace Bell remind you, each time it is rung, of our shared responsibility to build a more peaceful world.

📚 Books For Kids

Founder · International Project Coordinator · 2020–2025

An international cooperation between the Rotary Club of Wodonga West, the Rotary Club of Bangkok and the Rotary Club of Surin.

The project resulted in the shipment of thousands of high-quality English-language picture books from charity shops in Wodonga, Australia, to primary schools throughout Surin Province, Thailand.

💧 Anuban Krasang Water Sustainability Project

Project Designer · Project Coordinator · Feasibility Stage

Rotary ISAN – International and the Rotary Club of Krasang are developing a long-term sustainability project for Anuban Krasang School in Buriram Province.

The proposed project combines reliable water storage, solar-powered pumping and environmental education into one sustainable community initiative.

The project is currently seeking both local and international financial support from Rotary clubs, businesses and philanthropic organisations.

🦽 Community Wheelchair Initiative

Project Coordinator

Working together with the Rotary Club of Krasang, Rotary ISAN – International assists local communities by providing wheelchairs to residents whose independence and quality of life can be significantly improved through greater mobility.
